name: Raumschach
author: Dr. Ferdinand Maack (1907) / AGP port
description: Raumschach (German for "Space Chess") — invented by Dr. Ferdinand Maack in 1907 and the classic, oldest surviving three-dimensional chess variant (a Recognized Chess Variant). Played on five stacked 5×5 levels (A–E) forming a 5×5×5 cube. Rooks, bishops and knights move as in chess but extended through the third dimension; a new piece, the Unicorn, slides along the 3-D space-diagonals (corners of the cells); the Queen combines Rook + Bishop + Unicorn and the King moves the same, one step. Pawns advance forward or upward and capture diagonally. No castling, no two-step pawn move, no en passant. Win by checkmate.
